items.title: China’s Trip.com faces revenue slowdown, warns of ‘significant fine’ from antitrust probe
items.summary:
Trip.com Group, China’s largest online travel agency, says it expects second-quarter revenue growth to be the slowest in more than three years and warns of a “significant fine” from an ongoing antitrust probe by the country’s top market regulator.

Southbound Stock Connect flows surge to record US$152b driven by Hong Kong’s IPO revival
items.summary:
Southbound Stock Connect flows, through which mainland Chinese investors buy Hong Kong listed shares, hit a record high in the past year, reflecting strong confidence in the city’s market on the back of a booming pipeline of initial public offerings (IPO).
In the 12 months to March this year, mainland investors snapped up HK$1.19 trillion (US$151.8 billion) worth of shares in Hong Kong, according
